2604.05605.title: INTERACT: An AI-Driven Extended Reality Framework for Accesible Communication Featuring Real-Time Sign Language Interpretation and Emotion Recognition
2604.05605.paper_summary: Video conferencing has become central to professional collaboration, yet most platforms offer limited support for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and multilingual users. The World Health Organisation estimates that over 430 million people worldwide require rehabilitation for disabling hearing loss, a figure projected to exceed 700 million by 2050. Conventional accessibility measures remain constrained by high costs, limited availability, and logistical barriers, while Extended Reality (XR) technologies open new possibilities for immersive and inclusive communication. This paper presents INTERACT (Inclusive Networking for Translation and Embodied Real-Time Augmented Communication Tool), an AI-driven XR platform that integrates real-time speech-to-text conversion, International Sign Language (ISL) rendering through 3D avatars, multilingual translation, and emotion recognition within an immersive virtual environment. Built on the CORTEX2 framework and deployed on Meta Quest 3 headsets, INTERACT combines Whisper for speech recognition, NLLB for multilingual translation, RoBERTa for emotion classification, and Google MediaPipe for gesture extraction. Pilot evaluations were conducted in two phases, first with technical experts from academia and industry, and subsequently with members of the deaf community. The trials reported 92% user satisfaction, transcription accuracy above 85%, and 90% emotion-detection precision, with a mean overall experience rating of 4.6 out of 5.0 and 90% of participants willing to take part in further testing. The results highlight strong potential for advancing accessibility across educational, cultural, and professional settings. An extended version of this work, including full pilot data and implementation details, has been published as an Open Research Europe article [Tantaroudas et al., 2026a].
2604.05605.paper_summary_zh: 視訊會議已成為專業協作的核心，但大多數平台對於聽障、重聽和多語言使用者的支援有限。世界衛生組織估計，全球有超過4.3億人需要因殘疾性聽力損失而進行康復，這一數字預計到2050年將超過7億。傳統的無障礙措施受到高成本、有限可用性和後勤障礙的限制，而擴增實境（XR）技術則為沉浸式和包容性溝通開辟了新可能。本文介紹了INTERACT（包容性翻譯和具身實時增強溝通工具），這是一個基於AI的XR平台，整合了實時語音轉文字、通用手語（ISL）透過3D虛擬角色的呈現、多語言翻譯和情感識別，並在沉浸式虛擬環境中運行。INTERACT基於CORTEX2框架，並在Meta Quest 3頭戴式顯示器上部署，結合了Whisper進行語音識別、NLLB進行多語言翻譯、RoBERTa進行情感分類，以及Google MediaPipe進行手勢提取。試點評估分兩個階段進行，首先與來自學術界和行業的技術專家進行，隨後與聽障社群成員進行。試驗報告顯示92%的用戶滿意度，轉錄準確率超過85%，情感檢測精確度為90%，整體體驗平均評分為4.6（滿分5.0），90%的參與者願意參加進一步的測試。結果突顯了在教育、文化和專業環境中推進無障礙的強大潛力。這項工作的擴展版本，包括完整的試點數據和實施細節，已作為Open Research Europe文章發表[Tantaroudas et al., 2026a]。
2604.05605.author: Nikolaos D. Tantaroudas et.al.
2604.05605.authors: Nikolaos D. Tantaroudas, Andrew J. McCracken, Ilias Karachalios, Evangelos Papatheou
